Newly diagnosed right breast carcinoma; evaluation for systemic
therapy and coordination of multidisciplinary treatment.

Ms. Margaret Sullivan is a 58-year-old postmenopausal woman who
presents for evaluation of a newly diagnosed right breast cancer.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY
- Essential hypertension
- Osteopenia
- Right breast carcinoma (newly diagnosed, as above)

Ms. Sullivan is a 58-year-old postmenopausal woman with newly
diagnosed clinical stage IIB (cT2 cN1 cM0) invasive ductal
carcinoma of the right breast, Nottingham grade 2, strongly
hormone-receptor positive (ER 95%, PR 80%) and HER2 negative.

     1.1 cm and 0.6 cm), with associated treatment effect.

citations.text:
Ms. Sullivan is a 60-year-old postmenopausal woman with a history
of right breast invasive ductal carcinoma (originally clinical
stage IIB, ER/PR positive, HER2 negative), treated with neoadjuvant
chemotherapy followed by right partial mastectomy and sentinel node
biopsy in December 2025, adjuvant radiation, and adjuvant anastrozole
since February 2026.

PAST ONCOLOGIC HISTORY
- Right breast invasive ductal carcinoma, diagnosed 09/2025,
  cT2 cN1 cM0 (stage IIB), Nottingham grade 2, ER+/PR+/HER2-.

BIOMARKER AND PATHOLOGY REVIEW  (right breast core biopsy, 09/04/2025)
- Histology:           Invasive ductal carcinoma
- Histologic grade:    Nottingham grade 2 (score 6/9)
- Estrogen receptor:   POSITIVE, strong nuclear staining in 95% of
                       tumor cells (Allred 8/8).

CT-guided biopsy
of the L3 lesion on 02/03/2027 revealed metastatic adenocarcinoma
consistent with breast primary, ER positive, PR positive, HER2
negative — concordant with her original tumor profile.

extraction_reasoning: Estrogen receptor status is a key oncology tumor biomarker that determines targeted therapy decisions.
merge_reasoning: tumor marker: merged 2 ER mentions into one observation

issues.message: profile asserted but not validated locally: http://hl7.org/fhir/us/mcode/StructureDefinition/mcode-tumor-marker-test
